summary: Keep CP-016 emitter-local: the right seam is threading delegated-handle metadata through secret access decisions, not changing parsing/checking. The delegated handoff runtime fixture already proves the user-visible behavior end to end.
ergonomics: `src/Clasp/Emit/JavaScript.hs` keeps the secret delegation and audit helpers close together, which makes this kind of control-plane/runtime slice feasible without touching earlier compiler stages.
`test/Main.hs` already had a delegated secret handoff scenario, so extending that fixture was faster and safer than building a new benchmark or example.
Emitter string assertions are fragile when helper signatures gain optional parameters; they need to move with the runtime change.
follow_ups: Add a shared normalization/enforcement helper for delegation attenuation if later tasks need TTL/max-use semantics to affect resolution, not just auditing.
Add one explicit audit-log runtime assertion for routed delegated secret events after `auditRuntime.record(...)` so provenance and redaction stay covered together.
warnings: Thread provenance through both layers: secret consumers resolve delegated handles, but secret-input helpers actually build the decision payload. Updating only one silently drops delegation metadata.
`cabal test` outside `nix develop` fails here because `ghc` is not on PATH; use the nix shell or go straight to `bash scripts/verify-all.sh`.
tests_run: nix develop -c bash -lc 'cabal test --test-options='"'"'--pattern="delegated secret handoffs pass handles across agent tool and workflow boundaries without raw values"'"'"''
residual_risks: Delegation attenuation data is now preserved in audit traces, but the runtime still snapshots caller-supplied attenuation fields rather than validating or enforcing them during secret consumption.

Direct host calls to low-level `secretInput.decideAccess`/`resolve` only include delegation provenance if they pass the new optional runtime `options`; compiler-emitted consumer surfaces do this, but ad hoc host code must opt in.
